title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement jorgeroa@stanford.edu. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. Code Conduct ggpop package based Code Conduct used dplyr package Posit Software, PBC (formerly RStudio). grateful leadership fostering inclusive welcoming open-source communities. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: Turn numbers people. Turn data stories. ggpop R package built top ggplot2 simplifies creation engaging, icon-based population charts. combining features ggplot2 ggimage, ggpop lets users easily visualize population data using proportional, customizable icons arranged intuitive, circular layouts. package also includes functionality adding clear, icon-enhanced captions, makes charts easier understand visually attractive. Designed primarily visual storytelling, ggpop helps users communicate population statistics straightforward appealing manner.

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: ggpop offers fresh alternative traditional data visualizations using icons proportional symbols population charts. approach improves aesthetics plots also helps audience better engage understand data. converting numerical values intuitive visual representations, ggpop makes complex population data clearer easier remember, allowing users tell compelling accessible stories.

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: Visual Impact: Icons create immediate emotional connection audience. Intuitive Understanding: Proportional representation simplifies data. Flexible: Support 2,000+ Font Awesome icons. Fast: Optimized rendering handles 1,000 icons smoothly geom_pop() unlimited geom_icon_point() ggplot2 Native: Integrates seamlessly existing workflow — themes, facets, scales .

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: ggpop two principal geoms. solves different visualization problem:

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: can install ggpop CRAN : Development version package can installed GitHub :
code: install.packages("ggpop") install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("jurjoroa/ggpop")

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: geom_pop() creates proportional icon grids icon represents share total population. Perfect making demographic, health, social statistics nice format.

what: 1.- Create a Small Dataset or Use a Built-in Dataset
title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: dataset df_pop_mx minimal example illustrating population counts sex Mexico 2024. following structure: sex: categorical variable indicating sex ("male" / "female") n: numeric variable representing population size sex category country: constant value "Mexico" continent: constant value "America"
code: library(dplyr) library(ggpop)  df_pop_mx <- data.frame(sex = c("male", "female"),                         n = c(63459580, 67401427),                         country = "Mexico",                         continent = "America")

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: apply process_data() function population data df_pop_mx following parameters: group_var = sex: groups data sex (male/female). grouping variable sum_var = n: uses column n (population counts) group totals. variable summed calculate proportions. sample_size = 1000: generates 1,000 sampled records, proportionally allocated group. package allows sample size 1000. function calculates group proportions, performs sampling create new data frame (df_pop_mx_prop). row represents one draw 1,000 samples. Notable columns: type: group (male female) sampled. n: total population count corresponding group. prop: proportion group overall dataset. Note: process_data() optional. can pass data frame directly geom_pop() — long row represents one icon. maximum 1,000 rows per plot (can pass per facet group).
code: df_pop_mx_prop <- process_data(data = df_pop_mx, group_var = sex, sum_var = n, sample_size = 1000)

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: , create new column called icon df_pop_mx_prop dataset. case_when() function checks row’s type (either “male” “female”) assigns matching value icon column.
code: df_pop_mx_prop <- df_pop_mx_prop %>%   mutate(icon = case_when(     type == "male" ~ "male",     type == "female" ~ "female"))

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: package supports Font Awesome icons. Font Awesome icons offer greater flexibility broader icon set (2,000+ free icons). icons stored fontawesome package. thing need specify icon’s name. example, small sample 2,000+ free icons available: home · user · envelope · bell · camera · cog · heart · calendar · cart-plus · check · cloud · comment · download · edit · file · filter · flag · folder · phone  can check full list icons Font Awesome website.

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: Now can proceed plot population chart using assigned icons. geom_pop() function creates population chart using df_pop_mx_prop dataset. object works geom_point() figure plotted determined x y coordinates. can also group color icons type variable variable grouping .
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot() +   geom_pop(data = df_pop_mx_prop, aes(icon = icon, group = type, color = type),            size = 1, arrange = F, legend_icons = F) +   theme_void() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

previous_headings: geom_pop() — Population Charts > 5.- Plot population chart
title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: Like ggplot object, can layer themes, colors, titles, legend make chart presentation-ready. can also include two icons plot. example, identify people disabled, change parameters.
code: ggplot(data = df_pop_mx_prop, aes(icon = icon, group = type, color = type)) +   geom_pop(size = 1, arrange = T) +   theme_void(base_size = 40) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom") +   labs(title = "Population in Mexico by Sex",        subtitle = "2024",        caption = "Source: demogmx") +   theme(legend.title = element_blank(),         plot.background = element_blank(),         panel.background = element_blank(),         legend.background = element_blank(),         legend.text = element_text(color = "#D4AF37"),         plot.title = element_text(color = "#D4AF37"),         plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "#D4AF37"),         plot.caption = element_text(color = "#D4AF37")) +   scale_legend_icon(size = 10) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("male" = "#1E88E5", "female" = "#D81B60"),                      labels = c("female" = "Females: 51%", "male" = "Males: 49%")) #1.- We load or create the data df_pop_dis_mx <- data.frame(sex = c("male", "female", "disabled males", "disabled females"),                             value = c(53726732, 54978806, 9731396, 11106712),                             country = "Mexico",                             continent = "America")  #2.- We process the data df_pop_dis_mx_prop <- process_data(data = df_pop_dis_mx, group_var = sex,                                    sum_var = value, sample_size = 500)  #3.- Assign icons to groups df_pop_dis_mx_prop <- df_pop_dis_mx_prop %>%   mutate(icon = case_when(     type == "male" ~ "male",     type == "female" ~ "female",     type == "disabled males" ~ "wheelchair",     type == "disabled females" ~ "wheelchair"))  #4.- Plot  library(showtext) font_add_google("Quicksand", "quicksand") showtext_auto()  ggplot(data = df_pop_dis_mx_prop, aes(icon = icon, group = type, color = type)) +   geom_pop(size = 1.1, arrange = F) +   theme_pop(base_size = 100, base_family = "quicksand") +   scale_legend_icon(size = 10,                     legend.text = element_text(color = "#D4AF37", family = "quicksand"),                     plot.title = element_text(color = "#D4AF37", family = "quicksand",                                               face = "bold", size = 90, hjust = 0.5),                     plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "#D4AF37", family = "quicksand",                                                  size = 70, hjust = 0.5),                     plot.caption = element_text(color = "#D4AF37", family = "quicksand",                                                 size = 70, hjust = 0)) +   labs(title = "Population in Mexico by Sex and disability status",        subtitle = "2023",        caption = "As of 2023, 16% of the population in Mexico has some form of disability.") +   theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.title = element_blank(),         legend.box.spacing = unit(-.4, "cm"),         legend.margin = margin(t = 0, b = 0),         legend.box.margin = margin(t = 0, b = 0)) +   scale_color_manual(values = c("male" = "#1E88E5", "female" = "#D81B60",                                 "disabled males" = "#90CAF9",                                 "disabled females" = "#F48FB1"),                      labels = c("male" = "Males", "female" = "Females",                                 "disabled females" = "Disabled Females",                                 "disabled males" = "Disabled Males"))

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: geom_icon_point() scatter plot cousin geom_pop(). works exactly like geom_point() — swaps dots Font Awesome icons. preprocessing required: just pass data x y variables let icons talking. ideal want add visual identity individual observations — making audience immediately recognize looking , just point sits chart.

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: process_data() step needed — works raw data Icons placed freely x / y coordinates, arranged grid icon = one row dataset (population share) Supports mapped icons: different categories can show different icons

what: Example 1: Diet & Health Outcomes by Food Group
title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: point food item plotted calorie protein content. Every food gets icon — apple, banana, orange, drumstick, bacon, fish, bottle-water, cheese, jar — color groups category.
code: library(ggplot2) library(ggpop)  df_food <- data.frame(   food     = c("Apple", "Carrot", "Orange", "Chicken", "Beef", "Salmon",                "Milk", "Cheese", "Yogurt"),   calories = c(52, 41, 47, 165, 250, 208, 61, 402, 59),   protein  = c(0.3, 1.1, 0.9, 31, 26, 20, 3.2, 25, 10),   group    = c(rep("Fruit", 3), rep("Meat", 3), rep("Dairy", 3)),   icon     = c("apple-whole", "carrot", "lemon",                "drumstick-bite", "bacon", "fish",                "bottle-water", "cheese", "jar") )  ggplot(df_food, aes(x = calories, y = protein, icon = icon, color = food)) +   geom_icon_point(size = 2, dpi = 100) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(     "Apple" = "#FF5252", "Carrot" = "#FFA726", "Orange" = "#FFB74D",     "Chicken" = "#8D6E63", "Beef" = "#6D4C41",     "Salmon" = "#EF5350", "Milk" = "#42A5F5", "Cheese" = "#FFD54F",      "Yogurt" = "#4DB6AC"   )) +   labs(     title = "Calories vs. Protein by Food Group",     subtitle = "Each icon represents a specific food; color reflects the group",     x = "Calories (per 100g)",     y = "Protein (g per 100g)",     color = "Food Group"   )

what: Example 2: Tech Brand Revenue vs. Market Cap
title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: brand gets icon color. Icon size mapped number employees — bigger companies appear larger chart, adding third dimension information glance.
code: library(ggplot2) library(ggpop)  df_brand <- data.frame(   brand      = c("Apple", "Google", "Microsoft", "Meta", "Amazon",                  "Netflix", "Spotify", "Uber", "Airbnb"),   revenue    = c(394, 283, 212, 117, 514, 32, 13, 37, 9),   market_cap = c(2950, 1750, 2800, 1200, 1750, 190, 55, 140, 75),   employees  = c(160, 180, 220, 86, 1540, 13, 9, 32, 6),   sector     = c("Hardware", "Search", "Cloud", "Social", "Commerce",                  "Streaming", "Streaming", "Mobility", "Mobility"),   icon       = c("apple", "google", "windows", "meta", "amazon",                  "tv", "spotify", "uber", "airbnb") )  df_brand <- scales::rescale(df_brand, to = c(0.8, 2.5))  ggplot(df_brand, aes(x = revenue, y = market_cap,                        icon = icon, color = brand, size = size_scaled)) +   geom_icon_point(dpi = 120) +   scale_x_log10(labels = scales::dollar_format(suffix = "B")) +   scale_y_log10(labels = scales::dollar_format(suffix = "B")) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(     "Apple"     = "#FF5252", "Google"    = "#42A5F5",     "Microsoft" = "#4DB6AC", "Meta"      = "#8E24AA",     "Amazon"    = "#FFB300", "Netflix"   = "#E53935",     "Spotify"   = "#1DB954", "Uber"      = "#546E7A",     "Airbnb"    = "#FF4081")) +   scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 3), labels = scales::comma) +   labs(     title    = "Tech Giants: Revenue vs. Market Cap",     subtitle = "Size = employees (millions)  ·  Log scales",     x        = "Annual Revenue (log scale)",     y        = "Market Cap (log scale)",     color    = "Brand",     size     = "Employees (M)"   )

what: Featured Example: More Spending ≠ Longer Lives
title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: example shows geom_icon_point() combination five geoms — geom_smooth(), geom_vline(), geom_hline(), geom_text(), annotate() — build fully annotated analytical chart. icons encode income group visually (hospital high-income countries, stethoscope upper-middle, pills lower-middle low), geom_text() labels every country directly icon background, trend line, reference lines, quadrant annotations analytical heavy lifting. result chart rigorous immediately readable.

title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: geom_pop() integrates natively ggplot2’s faceting system, letting compare populations across groups geographies without extra setup. Just add facet parameter standard facet_wrap() / facet_grid() call, ggpop handles rest. even examples, vignettes, full function reference, visit ggpop package website.

what: facet_wrap() — Transportation Methods Across US Cities
title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: Using facet_wrap(~ group), chart breaks daily commute mix across major US cities. panel shows one city’s full distribution transportation modes — car, bus, train, bicycle, motorcycle, walking, ride-share — icon representing approximately 400 commuters. dark background per-mode color coding make easy compare cities glance. Code available ggpop package website.

what: facet_geo() — Gun Violence Across US States
title: Visualizing Population Data (ggpop)
text: Combining geom_pop() geofacet package places state’s panel actual geographic position US map. , skull icons represent gun deaths per 100,000 people (2023 CDC data), icon equal 2,000 people. layout immediately reveals regional patterns standard bar chart hide — Mississippi sits nearly 8× rate Massachusetts, South rural West cluster visually hardest-hit regions. Code available ggpop package website.

what: Create a scatter plot with Font Awesome icons instead of points — geom_icon_point
title: Create a scatter plot with Font Awesome icons instead of points — geom_icon_point
text: Works exactly like geom_point(), renders Font Awesome icons instead dots. Pass data x y variables - special formatting required.

title: Create a scatter plot with Font Awesome icons instead of points — geom_icon_point
code: geom_icon_point(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   stat = "identity",   position = "identity",   na.rm = FALSE,   inherit.aes = TRUE,   icon = NULL,   size = 1,   dpi = 50,   legend_icons = TRUE,   stroke_width = NULL,   ... )

title: Create a scatter plot with Font Awesome icons instead of points — geom_icon_point
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created aes(). specified inherit.aes = TRUE (default), combined default mapping top level plot. must supply mapping plot mapping. data data displayed layer. three options: NULL, default, data inherited plot data specified call ggplot(). data.frame, object, override plot data. objects fortified produce data frame. See fortify() variables created. function called single argument, plot data. return value must data.frame, used layer data. function can created formula (e.g. ~ head(.x, 10)). stat statistical transformation use data layer. using geom_*() function construct layer, stat argument can used override default coupling geoms stats. stat argument accepts following: Stat ggproto subclass, example StatCount. string naming stat. give stat string, strip function name stat_ prefix. example, use stat_count(), give stat "count". information ways specify stat, see layer stat documentation. position position adjustment use data layer. can used various ways, including prevent overplotting improving display. position argument accepts following: result calling position function, position_jitter(). method allows passing extra arguments position. string naming position adjustment. give position string, strip function name position_ prefix. example, use position_jitter(), give position "jitter". information ways specify position, see layer position documentation. na.rm logical, whether remove NA values inherit.aes FALSE, overrides default aesthetics, rather combining . useful helper functions define data aesthetics inherit behaviour default plot specification, e.g. annotation_borders(). icon Default Font Awesome icon (default: NULL). size Default icon size (default: 1). dpi Icon resolution (default: 50). legend_icons Show icons legend (default: TRUE). stroke_width Numeric. Width icon outline/stroke. ... additional parameters

title: Create a scatter plot with Font Awesome icons instead of points — geom_icon_point
text: ggplot layer.

title: Create a scatter plot with Font Awesome icons instead of points — geom_icon_point
text: geom_icon_point uses standard ggplot2 scatter plot aesthetics: x - Numeric variable x-axis y - Numeric variable y-axis icon - Font Awesome icon name (optional, column mapped) color/colour - Color grouping alpha - Transparency size - Icon size

title: Create a scatter plot with Font Awesome icons instead of points — geom_icon_point
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2) data <- data.frame(   x = rnorm(20),   y = rnorm(20),   category = sample(c("A", "B", "C"), 20, replace = TRUE),   icon = sample(c("heart", "star", "circle"), 20, replace = TRUE) )  # Map icon to a column ggplot(data, aes(x = x, y = y, icon = icon, color = category)) +   geom_icon_point()  # Use a fixed icon ggplot(data, aes(x = x, y = y, color = category)) +   geom_icon_point(icon = "star") } # }

title: Create a circular representative population chart — geom_pop
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created aes(). specified inherit.aes = TRUE (default), combined default mapping top level plot. must supply mapping plot mapping. data data displayed layer. three options: NULL, default, data inherited plot data specified call ggplot(). data.frame, object, override plot data. objects fortified produce data frame. See fortify() variables created. function called single argument, plot data. return value must data.frame, used layer data. function can created formula (e.g. ~ head(.x, 10)). stat statistical transformation use data layer. using geom_*() function construct layer, stat argument can used override default coupling geoms stats. stat argument accepts following: Stat ggproto subclass, example StatCount. string naming stat. give stat string, strip function name stat_ prefix. example, use stat_count(), give stat "count". information ways specify stat, see layer stat documentation. position position adjustment use data layer. can used various ways, including prevent overplotting improving display. position argument accepts following: result calling position function, position_jitter(). method allows passing extra arguments position. string naming position adjustment. give position string, strip function name position_ prefix. example, use position_jitter(), give position "jitter". information ways specify position, see layer position documentation. na.rm logical, whether remove NA values show.legend logical. layer included legends? NA, default, includes aesthetics mapped. FALSE never includes, TRUE always includes. can also named logical vector finely select aesthetics display. include legend keys levels, even data exists, use TRUE.  NA, levels shown legend, unobserved levels omitted. inherit.aes FALSE, overrides default aesthetics, rather combining . useful helper functions define data aesthetics inherit behaviour default plot specification, e.g. annotation_borders(). icon Default icon use icon column mapped. group_var (Deprecated) Use aes(group = ...) instead. sample_size total number individuals (points) draw. arrange Logical; TRUE, output data arranged group. seed Optional numeric seed used arrange = FALSE. sum_var Optional variable sum instead counting. facet Optional faceting variable. provided, final plot must faceted ggplot2 (use validate_geom_pop_faceting(p)). size Icon size. mapped aes(size = ...) parameter ignored. dpi Height (pixels) rendered PNG using fontawesome::fa_png(). Higher values produce sharper icons. legend_icons Logical; TRUE, legend displays selected icons. stroke_width Numeric. Width icon outline pixels (single value). ... additional parameters

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2)  df <- data.frame(   sex  = rep(c("F", "M"), each = 10),   icon = rep(c("female", "male"), each = 10) )  ggplot() +   geom_pop(     data = df,     aes(icon = icon, group = sex, color = sex),     size = 3,     dpi = 80   ) } # }

what: Process Population Data for Visualization — process_data
title: Process Population Data for Visualization — process_data
text: process_data function processes dataset calculate group proportions generates sampled dataset based specified parameters. processed data suitable creating visual representations, population charts, sample represents group associated counts proportions.

title: Process Population Data for Visualization — process_data
code: process_data(   data,   high_group_var = NULL,   group_var,   sum_var = NULL,   sample_size = 100 )

title: Process Population Data for Visualization — process_data
text: data data frame containing population data processed. high_group_var Character vector, optional. variables used group individuals hierarchically. categorical variable. provided, function samples individuals within group defined variables. group_var Quosure. variable used group individuals dataset. categorical variable. sum_var Quosure, optional. variable sum within group. NULL, function counts number individuals per group. sample_size Integer. total number individuals sample based group proportions. Must positive integer.

title: Process Population Data for Visualization — process_data
text: tibble (data frame) following columns: type sampled group type. group group identifier. n count individuals group. prop proportion group relative total population.

what: Legend helper for geom_pop/geom_icon_point legends — scale_legend_icon
title: Legend helper for geom_pop/geom_icon_point legends — scale_legend_icon
text: convenience function set appropriate legend key sizes icon-based legends. equivalent using theme(legend.key.size = ...) provides sensible defaults population icon plots.

title: Legend helper for geom_pop/geom_icon_point legends — scale_legend_icon
code: scale_legend_icon(   size = 10,   unit = "mm",   spacing = 0.2,   size_multiplier = 2,   ... )

title: Legend helper for geom_pop/geom_icon_point legends — scale_legend_icon
text: size Numeric. Legend key size specified units (default 10). unit Character. Unit legend key sizing (default "mm"). spacing Numeric. Spacing legend items fraction size (default 0.2). size_multiplier Numeric. Multiplier apply size spacing calculations (default 2). ... Additional theme arguments.

title: Legend helper for geom_pop/geom_icon_point legends — scale_legend_icon
text: ggplot2 theme object can added plot.

title: Legend helper for geom_pop/geom_icon_point legends — scale_legend_icon
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ggplot(df, aes(icon = icon, color = type)) +   geom_pop() +   scale_legend_icon(size = 20)  # Equivalent to: ggplot(df, aes(icon = icon, color = type)) +   geom_pop() +   theme(legend.key.size = unit(20, "mm")) } # }

title: Population Plot Theme — theme_pop
text: minimal theme optimized icon-based population plots. Similar theme_void() automatic legend key sizing, appropriate margins, sensible defaults population visualizations.

title: Population Plot Theme — theme_pop
code: theme_pop(   base_size = 11,   base_family = "",   base_line_size = base_size/22,   base_rect_size = base_size/22,   legend_icon_size = NULL,   legend_spacing = NULL,   plot_margin = NULL,   legend_position = "right" )

title: Population Plot Theme — theme_pop
text: base_size Base font size points (default: 11). base_family Base font family (default: ""). base_line_size Base size line elements (default: base_size/22). base_rect_size Base size rect elements (default: base_size/22). legend_icon_size Size legend icons cm. NULL (default), automatically calculated base_size/20 proportional sizing. legend_spacing Spacing legend items cm (default: 0.3 * legend_icon_size). plot_margin Plot margins. Default: margin(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 5.5, "pt"). Can single numeric (applied sides) margin() object. legend_position Position legend: "none", "left", "right", "bottom", "top" (default: "right").

title: Population Plot Theme — theme_pop
text: ggplot2 theme object.

title: Population Plot Theme — theme_pop
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage ggplot(data = df, aes(icon = icon, color = type)) +   geom_pop(size = 1) +   theme_pop()  # Large text with bottom legend ggplot(data = df, aes(icon = icon, color = type)) +   geom_pop(size = 1) +   theme_pop(base_size = 40, legend_position = "bottom") } # }

NULL (default), automatically calculated base_size/20 proportional sizing. legend_spacing Spacing legend items cm (default: 0.3 * legend_icon_size). plot_margin Plot margins. Default: margin(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 5.5, "pt"). Can single numeric (applied sides) margin() object. legend_position Position legend: "none", "left", "right", "bottom", "top" (default: "right"). bg_color Background color (default: "black"). text_color Text color (default: "white").

title: ggpop 1.7.0
text: release ggpop delivers new theming support, expanded icon customization, critical bug fixes, significant internal refactoring toward fully ggplot-native architecture. Deprecated functionality removed public API finalized.

title: ggpop 1.7.0
text: Fixed icon size inconsistency across different ggplot2 themes corrected scale_legend_icon() properly reflect theme settings (#287). Fixed legend icon ordering factor variables mapped colour, ensuring legend icons follow factor level order rather data row order (#294).

title: ggpop 1.7.0
text: Refactored internal geom functions fully ggplot-native, using plot-scoped layer computation removing reliance last_plot() layer construction (#266). Added robustness unit tests stroke_width parameter validate behavior across edge cases (#269). Expanded unit tests internal helper functions incorporated code improvements reliability maintainability (#275). Cleaned error warning messages removing file-level path details, replacing concise, user-facing descriptions (#277). Normalized icon size calculation ensure consistent rendering geom_pop geom_icon_point (#282). Added unit tests visual snapshot tests scale_legend_icon validate legend sizing rendering behavior (#289).

title: ggpop 1.7.0
text: Added stroke_width parameter geom_icon_point, allowing users control icon outline thickness improved visual contrast (#268). Introduced personalized ggpop themes (e.g., theme_pop) consistent, opinionated plot styling box (#291).

title: ggpop 1.7.0
text: Removed deprecated caption_pop function. Users migrate standard ggplot2 annotation approaches (#279). Finalized public function names key glyph implementations geom_pop geom_icon_point, standardizing API. Existing code relying previous internal names may require updates (#281).

title: ggpop 1.7.0
text: Issues listed chronological merge order. #266 #268 #269 #275 #277 #279 #281 #282 #287 #289 #291 #294

title: ggpop 1.6.1
text: release ggpop introduces set targeted updates addressing multiple reported issues related icon rendering, size handling, validation logic, internal refactoring. changes improve robustness, consistency, maintainability preserving expected user-facing behavior.

title: ggpop 1.6.1
text: Fixed icon column mapping geom_pop geom_icon_point, ensuring users can map icons custom column name without rendering errors unexpected behavior (#255). Fixed aspect ratio preservation legend icon rendering, ensuring icons scale intelligently within legend box maintaining proportions (#254).

title: ggpop 1.6.1
text: Refactored process_data function comprehensive input validation, enhanced error handling, improved calculation proportions, including proper handling NA values filtering invalid data (#243). Enhanced legend icon sizing logic automatically scale icons within legend box preserving aspect ratio, wide icons filling horizontally tall icons filling vertically (#253). Improved error warning message formatting throughout package better readability consistency, including multi-line formatting actionable guidance (#253). Added comprehensive unit tests process_data function covering various scenarios including hierarchical grouping, input validation, edge cases, reproducibility (#242). Improved code clarity structure across multiple functions refactoring argument formatting, removing unnecessary whitespace, adding thematic sections comments (#253). Enhanced cross-layer validation moving legend icon consistency checks plot-add hook, ensuring layers considered detecting mixed legend_icons settings (#258).

title: ggpop 1.6.1
text: Added new ggplot_add.ggpop_icon_point_layer method handle addition icon point layers consistency checks legend icon settings clear error messages (#258).

title: ggpop 1.6.1
text: Changed default size parameter 3 1 geom_icon_point geom_pop align new icon scaling logic. may affect existing plots relying previous default value (#254).

title: ggpop 1.6.1
text: Issues listed chronological merge order. #243 #242 #254 #255 #258 #253

title: ggpop 1.6.0
text: Fixed draw_key function prefer packaged PNG icons available cache icons temp directory, ensuring CI environment compatibility avoiding unnecessary regeneration (#189). Fixed arrange argument functionality geom_pop, ensuring proper icon arrangement behavior (#191). Fixed legend icon size rendering ensure consistent display across different scenarios (#199). Fixed facet_wrap bug ensure proper faceting behavior plots (#201). Fixed geom_pop work properly aesthetics specified main aes() call (#214). Fixed DPI parameter properly control icon rendering quality (#220). Fixed geom_icon_point DPI parameter wasn’t updating quality expected (#224). Fixed scale_legend_icon margin clipping issue prevent legend icons cut (#205). Fixed aspect ratio preservation icon rendering geom_pop draw_key_image functions (#230).

title: ggpop 1.6.0
text: Improved internal implementation removing reliance last_plot() pipeline geom_pop, enhancing code reliability avoiding potential side effects (#195). Enhanced unit tests improve code coverage reliability (#187). Improved README additional examples demonstrating package capabilities use cases, including faceting geofacet scenarios (#204). Updated unit tests robustness checks ensure stable behavior across different use cases (#216). Added comprehensive unit tests geom_icon_point ensure proper functionality (#223).

title: ggpop 1.6.0
text: Added new geom_icon_point function allow users plot data freely icons, providing greater flexibility beyond population-specific visualizations (#212). Added stroke_width parameter control thickness Font Awesome icons, enabling customization options (#217).

title: ggpop 1.5.0
text: release ggpop introduces set targeted updates geom_pop function R/geom_pop.R, addressing multiple reported issues related icon rendering, size handling, validation logic, internal refactoring. changes improve robustness, consistency, maintainability preserving expected user-facing behavior unless explicitly noted.

title: ggpop 1.5.0
text: Fixed legend icon assignment faceted plots, ensuring correct icon mapping faceting conditions introducing dynamic legend icon assignment (#168).

title: ggpop 1.5.0
text: Refactored input validation user guidance infrastructure introducing validation warning functions, improving error messages actionable fixes (#162). Enhanced icon rendering compatibility adding rsvg dependency improving legend customization support (#164). Refactored facet argument processing accept symbol string inputs (e.g., facet = sex facet = "sex"), improving flexibility (#166). Renamed quality parameter dpi clarity, indicating refers image pixel height rather subjective quality (#161). Enhanced input validation enforcing maximum 1000 icons per plot facet group, changing default sample_size 1000 100, renaming internal size variables avoid collisions (#152). Improved facet handling auto-inferred facet variables facet_wrap facet_grid, treating multiple groups internal facets explicit facet provided (#173). Enforced mandatory icon specification clear error messages, added hard stop dpi values 30 prevent blurry icons, enhanced warnings ambiguous input scenarios (#175). Improved error messages throughout actionable fixes examples, changed icon PNG file writing use temporary cache directory instead package directory, standardized internal column naming conventions (#179).

text: Added support raw dataframes without requiring process_data(), automatic mode detection type inference user convenience (#177).

title: ggpop 1.0.0
text: Fixed bug prevented proper arrangement plots facet_wrap facet_grid used. Addressed issues merging process x1 y1 columns missing merging data df_coordinates_final, ensuring consistent behavior. Resolved problem grouping variables faceted plots lead mismatches data merge step. Fixed fetch_df_coordinates function: Corrected cache directory assignment removing erroneous reference "yourpackage" ensuring correctly points "ggpop". ensures coordinate data properly cached retrieved, preventing potential errors related missing incorrect coordinate mappings.

title: ggpop 1.0.0
text: Improved handling facet variables dynamically calculating sample_size group ensuring consistent data types merging. Reduced redundancy code streamlining recalculation coordinates (df_coordinates_final) filtering (df_coordinates_filtered). Added robust handling grouped facet data, support facet_wrap facet_grid. Introduced legend_icons parameter geom_pop allow users include custom icons within legend without hiding entire legend. enhancement provides greater flexibility customizing legends, enabling display representative images alongside group labels informative visually appealing plots.

text: Customizable Icon Simulation: version package introduces ability simulate population groups originating different categories using customizable icons. Users can select variety icons import , tailoring visualization specific needs. Descriptive Icon-Adorned Captions: ggpop now includes tools adding descriptive captions adorned icons. captions provide textual information also incorporate icons visually represent different population groups, enhancing overall narrative visualization.
